exercises.title: GATEKEEPER
promptLines: Define **requirePositive(n: number): number** returning `n` when `n > 0`, otherwise **throwing** an Error.
starterLines: // TODO: throw when n <= 0, else return n
solutionLines: if (n <= 0) throw new Error('must be positive');
exercises.hint: if (n <= 0) throw new Error(...); return n;
exercises.lore: None pass the reactor gate without a positive charge.

exercises.title: CHECKPOINT
exercises.brief: Demand a real tag, trimmed clean.
promptLines: Define **validateTag(s: string): string** that **trims** surrounding whitespace and returns the result.
If the trimmed tag is empty (blank or all spaces), **throw** an Error instead.
starterLines: // TODO: trim s; throw if empty, else return it
exercises.hint: const t = s.trim(); if (t === '') throw new Error(...); return t;
exercises.lore: A blank ID badge gets you nowhere at the checkpoint.

exercises.title: FALLBACK CACHE
exercises.brief: Trust the function, keep a backup ready.
promptLines: Define a generic **getOrElse<T>(fn: () => T, fallback: T): T** that calls `fn()` and returns its value.
If `fn()` **throws**, swallow the error with `try` / `catch` and return `fallback` instead. It must never throw.
starterLines: function getOrElse<T>(fn: () => T, fallback: T): T {
// TODO: return fn(), or fallback if it throws
solutionLines: function getOrElse<T>(fn: () => T, fallback: T): T {
exercises.hint: try { return fn(); } catch (e) { return fallback; }
exercises.lore: When the live feed dies, the cache keeps the deck breathing.

exercises.title: RESULT ROOT
exercises.brief: Square root that returns a typed Result.
promptLines: A **Result union** carries either a value or an error, never both:
type Result<T> = { ok: true; value: T } | { ok: false; error: string };
Define **safeSqrt(n: number): Result<number>**. For negative `n` return `{ ok: false, error: 'negative' }`; otherwise return `{ ok: true, value: Math.sqrt(n) }`.
starterLines: type Result<T> = { ok: true; value: T } | { ok: false; error: string };
// TODO: negative -> error result, else ok result
solutionLines: type Result<T> = { ok: true; value: T } | { ok: false; error: string };
exercises.hint: if (n < 0) return { ok: false, error: 'negative' }; return { ok: true, value: Math.sqrt(n) };
exercises.lore: The result packet tags itself: a clean root, or a flagged fault.

exercises.title: FIRST CONTACT
exercises.brief: Return the first attempt that survives.
promptLines: Define a generic **firstOk<T>(fns: Array<() => T>): T** that tries each function in order and returns the value of the **first one that does not throw**.
Wrap each call in `try` / `catch`; if **every** function throws, **throw** an Error.
starterLines: // TODO: return the first call that does not throw; throw if all fail

tests.name: skips a thrower
tests.code: assertEqual(firstOk([function () { throw new Error('a'); }, function () { return 7; }]), 7);

tests.name: skips two throwers
tests.code: assertEqual(firstOk([function () { throw new Error('a'); }, function () { throw new Error('b'); }, function () { return 'live'; }]), 'live');

tests.name: all throw -> throws
tests.code: assertThrows(function () { firstOk([function () { throw new Error('x'); }, function () { throw new Error('y'); }]); });

exercises.hint: loop the array, try { return fns[i](); } catch (e) {}; after the loop throw new Error(...)
exercises.lore: Probe the array of channels; the first that answers clean is your link.
